rulings.Case Title: JADE CHAPMAN VS. CHOICE HOTELS INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 17, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT CHOICE HOTELS INTERNATIONAL, INC., COMFORT INN BY THE BAY MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts in support of request for punitive damages. Late-filed oversized opposition is stricken. The Court notes that, according to Plaintiff's counsel's declaration, opposition (16 pages) was prepared and filed "upon receipt of Defendants' Reply Re: Non-opposition on May 14, 2024." Defendant's reply was filed at 2:32 p.m. on May 14, 2024. Plaintiff's 16-page brief in opposition was filed 30 minutes later at 2:52 p.m. on May 14, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: RAMON CAMPOS-CEJA VS. PHH MORTGAGE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Phh Mortgage Corporation And Deutsche Bank National Trust Company, As Trustee'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 17, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT PHH MORTGAGE CORPORATION, DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE Defendant Phh Mortgage Corporation And Deutsche Bank National Trust Company, As Trustee'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration is GRANTED without leave to amend. Based on Plaintiff's allegations in the complaint the foreclosure sale was scheduled for April 3, 2024 and Plaintiff's application for medication is dated April 10, 2024, i.e. 7 days after the foreclosure sale. The gravamen of Defendants motion for judgment on the pleadings (addressing solely the 2nd, 3rd and 5th causes of action) is the submission of the application after the foreclosure. Plaintiff's opposition does not address or even acknowledge this argument in any way. The Court notes that opposition devoted 7.5 pages out of 10 pages (starting at section IV) to causes of action that are not subject to this motion. Sections of the opposition addressing the relevant causes of action did not address the arguments raised in the moving papers. Plaintiff provided no suggestions on how the complaint may be amended to state the 2nd, 3rd and 5th causes of action. The Court further notes that Plaintiff failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Responses By Defendant To Plaintiff'S Demand For Identification And Production Of Documents, Set One, And Production Without Objections, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 17, 2024 line 9. PLAINTIFF TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Responses By Defendant To Plaintiff'S Demand For Identification And Production Of Documents, Set One, And Production Without Objections, Etc. is continued to July 31, 2024 for Plaintiff to provide a supplemental brief and a supplemental declaration. Supplemental brief shall address the sufficiency of the proof of service that does not include the name of the declarant (See Proof of Service attached at Ex. E to Tong declaration and Proof of Service filed on July 9, 2024). Supplemental brief shall not exceed 3 pages and shall address, in addition to any other authorities Plaintiff relies on, case law discussing the Court's ability to hold the unnamed person in contempt should any issues of veracity of the declaration arise. rulings.Case Title: LAN LE VS. PATRICK DOMINGO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 17, 2024 line 11. DEFENDANT PATRICK DOMINGO, KIRSTEN DOMINGO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposition party failed to show that triable issues of fact exist. The operative notice is fatally defective as it fails to notify the tenant of the legally proper deadline to submit the payment to cure the default in rent. Defendant was entitled to know that defendant had 3 days excluding weekends and holiday to tender the payment, not just three days. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
